description: Status of major security threats in Nigeria as of May 2026.

data.current_trend: Persistent; organizational continuity despite territorial losses. Revival noted after Shekau death (2021).
data.electoral_impact: High voter suppression effect in Borno, Yobe, and parts of Adamawa. These states had lower-than-national-average turnout in 2023.

data.current_trend: Active around Lake Chad basin; enemies with Boko Haram (JAS) since 2021
data.electoral_impact: Overlaps with Boko Haram impact; Borno and Yobe elections require heavy security deployment

data.current_trend: Highly active. Wikipedia banditry article returned 404; data from general knowledge.
data.electoral_impact: Significant impact on NW voter registration and turnout. Zamfara, Kaduna particularly affected.
data.data_gap: Banditry Wikipedia article returned 404. Operator should fetch ACLED Nigeria data.

data.current_trend: Intensifying. 2023: 200+ deaths in Dec holiday attacks in Plateau. 2025: 100+ deaths in Benue.
data.electoral_impact: IDP displacement affects voter registration; security crisis may depress turnout in affected LGAs. Politically polarizes Muslim/Christian communities in NC.

data.current_trend: Sit-at-home orders Mondays disrupting commerce; ESN (Eastern Security Network) armed wing active. Simon Ekpa (Finland-based) continues calling sit-at-home orders.
data.electoral_impact: Monday sit-at-home orders directly affect election days (may be called off for elections or enforced). 2023 SE elections saw some violence but proceeded.
data.data_gap: IPOB Wikipedia article not fetched in this pass. Operator should add.

gaps.note: ACLED data (acleddata.com/data) has monthly incident counts by state — not retrieved.
Most recent 2024-2026 incident data not retrieved for any group.
